nodes.label: Squirrel Project Conventions (LID+EARS)
nodes.rationale: Spec-driven development using LID+EARS documentation chain: HLD -> LLD -> EARS -> code/tests. Do NOT use OpenSpec scaffolding.

nodes.label: server.py — Squirrel Web UI (Python JSON API + React SPA)
nodes.norm_label: server.py — squirrel web ui (python json api + react spa)

nodes.label: Installation Method: Copy vs Symlink
nodes.rationale: USE_LINK flag: 0=copy (stable), 1=symlink (auto-updates). Copy is recommended for stability; symlink for developers.

nodes.label: Multi-Vault Configuration Architecture
nodes.rationale: Spec: docs/ears/multi-vault-core.md. Single config (~/.squirrel/config.toml) stores [[vaults]] array with name/path/default. Per-vault state files in ~/.squirrel/state/<vault_name>.json

nodes.label: SQUIRREL-PACKAGE Protocol (v1) — Hash-Verified Context Transfer
nodes.rationale: Spec: SYNC-007, SYNC-002, SYNC-003. Self-contained Markdown block with SHA-256 hash for air-gapped environment transfers. Detects sensitive content before packaging.

nodes.label: PARA Vault Schema (01-Proyectos / 02-Parking / 03-Areas / 06-Archive)
nodes.rationale: Vault structure: projects grouped by location (WIP/Parking/Areas/Archive). Each project is a folder with Project Page (.md file named after folder) + intent files.

nodes.label: Intent Frontmatter YAML Schema
nodes.rationale: Spec: VAULT-008. Simple YAML subset (no external deps): scalars (id: TAG-001), inline lists (tags: [...]), block lists, quoted strings, ISO date strings.

nodes.label: Hook Specification (INT-007) — Normalized Payload Contract
nodes.rationale: Every hook command must expose env vars: EVENT (e.g., SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, Stop), PROJECT (project tag or empty), TIMESTAMP (ISO-8601 UTC).

nodes.label: SESSION-004 Guard — Project Ambiguity Resolution
nodes.rationale: If project cannot be unambiguously determined (no explicit arg, no recent activity, no cwd match), stop and ask user to pick. Never produce loading summary without confirmed project.

nodes.label: VAULT-007 Guard — Read-Only Vault Operations
nodes.rationale: where-am-i skill is read-only. Don't read intent files directly (script already did), don't write to vault, don't reformat output.

nodes.label: Web UI Server Constraint C1: Python Stdlib Only
nodes.rationale: Server side: Python stdlib only. UI side (React + Vite + Tailwind + shadcn) has its own Node deps. Atomic writes via temp file + os.replace.
nodes.norm_label: web ui server constraint c1: python stdlib only

nodes.label: SQLite notifications Table Schema
nodes.rationale: Central data structure for notification history with read/unread state tracking

nodes.label: Rust Daemon Notification Logic
nodes.rationale: Writes notifications to SQLite, emits Tauri events, manages OS popup guard

nodes.rationale: REST API for notification CRUD operations and settings persistence

nodes.rationale: User-facing controls for in_app and os_popups flags

nodes.label: In-App Notification Center Feature
nodes.rationale: Migrates notification experience from macOS system notifications to in-app center with persistent history

nodes.label: Obsidian Vault with Markdown Files

nodes.label: WAL Mode Concurrent Access
nodes.rationale: Allows Rust daemon writes and Python reads without locking

nodes.label: Notification Deduplication by Item + Date
nodes.rationale: Prevents duplicate rows when daemon polls every 30s for same active alert

nodes.rationale: Disabling in_app=false gates all notification system logic

nodes.label: OS Notifications Opt-In Default OFF
nodes.rationale: User requested migration away from OS popups

nodes.norm_label: notification write path: daemon to sqlite

nodes.rationale: Frontend listens to Tauri events, fetches /api/notifications, renders NotificationCenter

nodes.rationale: Settings written to config.toml, read by daemon on each poll cycle

nodes.label: GET /api/notifications
nodes.rationale: Fetch notifications with optional limit and unread filter

nodes.rationale: Mark all unread notifications as read

nodes.rationale: Mark single notification as read

nodes.rationale: Mark single notification as dismissed

nodes.label: POST /api/settings/notifications
nodes.rationale: Persist in_app and os_popups flags to config.toml

nodes.label: GET /api/me (notifications key)
nodes.rationale: Retrieve current notification settings for daemon polling

nodes.label: Tauri Event: squirrel:notif-updated
nodes.rationale: Emitted after INSERT with unread count payload triggers frontend refetch

nodes.rationale: Renders notification rows with Go To and dismiss buttons

nodes.rationale: No caching allows UI changes to take effect within 30s

nodes.rationale: Already appear in tray menu; would duplicate and clutter

nodes.rationale: Default interval for checking API endpoints and firing notifications

nodes.rationale: All fired alerts persist in SQLite across app restarts

nodes.label: Success Criterion: Tray Badge State Reflects Unread
nodes.rationale: Notification icon when unread > 0, Normal when all read/dismissed
nodes.norm_label: success criterion: tray badge state reflects unread

nodes.label: Success Criterion: Direct Note Navigation
nodes.rationale: Go To button opens browser at correct note URL

nodes.label: Success Criterion: Settings Take Effect Without Restart
nodes.rationale: Toggling OS notifications stops/starts popups immediately
nodes.norm_label: success criterion: settings take effect without restart
